game_name: Skate or Die 2: The Search for Double Trouble

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Used throughout all stages; core mechanic for defeating enemies and collecting goodies
Replaced by better boards acquired through Rodney
Alternative to paintball gun; found as pickups from defeated enemies
Requires collecting goodies (CDs, cassettes, food) to trade for tricks and boards; conditional appearance in later stages
Second upgrade for accessing higher platforms
Defeated via paintball gun; yield weapons and goodies
Showdown in warehouse; stage objective completion
Delivery missions with set time limits; reward/penalty based on speed
Non-hostile obstacles in shopping mall; navigation challenge
New enemy type; context-specific to mall setting
Blueprint pages scattered by fan; resource collection under environmental pressure
Non-replacing boards; speed vs. jump tradeoff options
Departure from linear side-scrolling; multi-room navigation required

typical_relief_triggers: Trading items with NPCs for upgrades
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple enemy types in rapid succession
Warehouse maze navigation without clear goal direction
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Adventure mode follows four distinct story-driven stages with escalating difficulty and narrative pressure; early stages allow exploration and resource gathering, middle stages introduce time pressure and environmental chaos, final stage constrains player to maze-based confrontation

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Returning to previously visited areas to meet Rodney/Lester for upgrades; optional skateboard variants available only if acquired early
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Conditional NPC appearances based on player's prior purchases; if Rodney/Lester items not acquired in Stage 1, they do not appear in Stage 3

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe introduction in Stage 1 (Elwood streets); basic enemies and simple 1-screen navigation; player learns paintball gun and bartering mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stage 2 (shopping mall) introduces time pressure and new obstacle types; Stage 3 (beach) escalates to environmental hazards and resource collection under deadline
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 4 (warehouse) shifts to 4-floor maze navigation; final confrontation with Icepick; requires mastery of all prior mechanics in constrained space
notable_spikes: Stage 2: First experience of time pressure in timed deliveries
Stage 3: Blueprint scattering creates multiple simultaneous collection objectives
Stage 4: Maze layout breaks established side-scrolling pattern; Icepick final boss

notable_level_patterns.pattern_name: Linear side-scrolling exploration with NPC hubs
notable_level_patterns.description: Stages 1-3 present long traversable screens allowing backtracking to Rodney/Lester for upgrades; enemies and goodies scattered throughout; stage objective clear but optional content (trick purchases) available via exploration
Stage 2 introduces deliveries with time limits; player must navigate obstacles (shoppers, security guard) while managing speed vs. safety; rewards (tips) tied to performance
Stage 3 uses physics-based scenario (fan scattering blueprints) to create urgent resource collection; wind hazard metaphorically suggests chaos and time pressure without explicit timer
Stage 4 breaks side-scrolling convention with 4-floor room-based navigation; constrains player movement to focused, vertical progression toward boss; rooftop final confrontation
